interpretation: Random mode is truly controlled by missing_rate; higher rate makes more samples rely on generated modalities, so sample classification drops.
Random denoise also drops because more samples enter the completed-fusion branch, although the generation diagnostic itself remains much easier than sample.
Fixed missing_eeg ignores missing_rate in mask construction; all samples have EEG missing, so rate changes only metadata, not the effective mask.
Fixed missing_eeg denoise is identical for both rates because the test mask is identical.
Fixed missing_eye ignores missing_rate in mask construction; tiny differences are sampling randomness, not a systematic missing_rate effect.
Fixed missing_eye denoise is identical for both rates because the test mask is identical.

delta_acc_0.5_minus_0.3: Average sample performance drops mildly from 0.3 to 0.5, mainly caused by random mode.
Average denoise performance drops mildly; fixed missing modes are unchanged, so the average decrease mainly comes from random mode.
